message.content:
I saw something odd go by in a chat: looks like there's a quoted string in a place where RCG recommends a naked string.  Do you see the issue?


⏺ Update(Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdro_onboarding.rs)
  ⎿  Added 6 lines, removed 20 lines
       89
       90  // ── Yoke fan-out — inscribe-reliquary yoking all ordain-side vessels ─────────
       91  //
       92 -// Yoke applies to conjure-mode vessels only — they are the ones whose
       93 -// Dockerfile pulls from the reliquary tool images at Cloud Build time, so
       94 -// they need the stamp written into rbrv.env. Bind-mode and graft-mode
       95 -// vessels skip Cloud Build (bind pins an upstream digest; graft pushes a
       96 -// pre-built image), so the reliquary toolchain is not in their build path.
       97 -// `rbfl_yoke` enforces this by refusing non-conjure vessels.
       98 -//
       99 -// ccyolo has RBRV_RELIQUARY= in its rbrv.env (present, empty) — it is a
      100 -// conjure-mode vessel whose Dockerfile pulls from the reliquary tool images,
      101 -// so it needs the stamp at ordain time. Included.
      102 -//
      103 -// rbev-bottle-plantuml has RBRV_RELIQUARY= as schema parity but is
      104 -// bind-mode. Excluded from yoke fan-out — bind mode does not use the
      105 -// reliquary toolchain.
      106 -//
      107 -// rbev-graft-demo also has RBRV_RELIQUARY= but graft-mode vessels do not use
      108 -// the reliquary toolchain (they push a pre-built image; no Dockerfile build).
      109 -// Excluded from yoke fan-out despite the presence of the field.
       92 +// All ordain-path vessels — conjure, bind, AND graft — require RBRV_RELIQUARY.
       93 +// The reliquary tool images feed the about/vouch pipeline in every mode
       94 +// (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft); bind also uses skopeo for image-copy. Yoke
       95 +// fan-out covers every vessel that ordain will visit.
       96
       97  const RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS: &[&str] = &[
       98      RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_SENTRY_TETHER,
       99      RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE,
      100      RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_BOTTLE,
      101      RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_JUPYTER,
      102 +    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_PLANTUML,
      103 +    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_GRAFT,
      104      "rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo",
      117 -    // rbev-bottle-plantuml excluded: bind mode does not use reliquary tool images
      118 -    // rbev-graft-demo excluded: graft mode does not use reliquary tool images
      105  ];
      106
      107  // ── Hallmark-base locator construction ───────────────────────

  Searched for 1 pattern, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

aiTitle: Fix quoted string in vessel directory constant

16	//
17	// RBTDRO — gauntlet onboarding-sequence fixture (§3 of release qualification)
18	//
19	// Each case walks the operator-facing onboarding handbook track for one
20	// vessel-construction mode, invoking the handbook's prescribed tabtargets
21	// in the prescribed order. Case order and per-case docs live with the
22	// functions below; the registered order is the source of truth (see the
23	// `cases:` array in RBTDRO_SECTIONS_ONBOARDING_SEQUENCE).
24	//
25	// Disposition: StateProgressing. Build-only — no charge, no test. Cases stop
26	// when each handbook-prescribed hallmark lands in GAR. Per-case precondition
27	// probes enable a-la-carte single-case rerun.
28	//
29	// inscribe_reliquary yokes the reliquary stamp into vessel rbrv.env files
30	// and commits. Downstream cases verify it ran by reading RBRV_RELIQUARY from
31	// a stable yoked vessel's rbrv.env — no out-of-source-tree scratch state.
32	
33	use std::io::{BufRead, BufReader, Write};
34	use std::path::{Path, PathBuf};
35	
36	use crate::case;
37	use crate::rbtdrb_probe::{rbtdrb_assert, rbtdrb_Probe};
38	use crate::rbtdrc_crucible::{rbtdrc_with_ctx, RBTDRC_FACT_HALLMARK, RBTDRC_FACT_RELIQUARY};
39	use crate::rbtdre_engine::{rbtdre_Disposition, rbtdre_Fixture, rbtdre_Section, rbtdre_Verdict};
40	use crate::rbtdri_invocation::{
41	    rbtdri_invoke_global, rbtdri_read_burv_fact, rbtdri_Context, rbtdri_InvokeResult,
42	};
43	use crate::rbtdrk_canonical::rbtdrk_canonical_rbra;
44	use crate::rbtdrm_manifest::{
45	    RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ENSHRINE_VESSEL, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY,
46	    RBTDRM_COLOPHON_KLUDGE_BOTTLE, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_KLUDGE_SENTRY, RBTDRM_COLOPHON_ORDAIN,
47	    RBTDRM_COLOPHON_YOKE_RELIQUARY, RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ONBOARDING_SEQUENCE,
48	};
49	
50	// ── Vessel directories ────────────────────────────────────────
51	
52	const RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_SENTRY_TETHER: &str = "rbev-vessels/rbev-sentry-deb-tether";
53	const RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE: &str = "rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge";
54	const RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_BOTTLE: &str = "rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap";
55	const RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_PLANTUML: &str = "rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-plantuml";
56	const RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_JUPYTER: &str = "rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-anthropic-jupyter";
57	const RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_GRAFT: &str = "rbev-vessels/rbev-graft-demo";
58	
59	// ── Nameplate monikers ────────────────────────────────────────
60	
61	const RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_TADMOR: &str = "tadmor";
62	const RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_CCYOLO: &str = "ccyolo";
63	const RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_MORIAH: &str = "moriah";
64	const RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_SRJCL: &str = "srjcl";
65	const RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_PLUML: &str = "pluml";
66	
67	// ── Nameplate hallmark variable names (lines in .rbk/<nameplate>/rbrn.env) ──
68	
69	const RBTDRO_HALLMARK_VAR_BOTTLE: &str = "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK";
70	const RBTDRO_HALLMARK_VAR_SENTRY: &str = "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK";
71	
72	// ── Consumer arrays ───────────────────────────────────────────
73	
74	/// Nameplates that receive the sentry-tether hallmark from ordain-conjure.
75	const RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_SENTRY_TETHER: &[&str] = &[
76	    RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_MORIAH,
77	    RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_SRJCL,
78	    RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_PLUML,
79	];
80	
81	/// Nameplates that receive the airgap-bottle hallmark from ordain-airgap.
82	const RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_AIRGAP_BOTTLE: &[&str] = &[RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_MORIAH];
83	
84	/// Nameplates that receive the plantuml-bottle hallmark from ordain-bind.
85	const RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_PLANTUML_BOTTLE: &[&str] = &[RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_PLUML];
86	
87	/// Nameplates that receive the jupyter-bottle hallmark from conjure-srjcl.
88	const RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_JUPYTER_BOTTLE: &[&str] = &[RBTDRO_NAMEPLATE_SRJCL];
89	
90	// ── Yoke fan-out — inscribe-reliquary yoking all ordain-side vessels ─────────
91	//
92	// All ordain-path vessels — conjure, bind, AND graft — require RBRV_RELIQUARY.
93	// The reliquary tool images feed the about/vouch pipeline in every mode
94	// (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft); bind also uses skopeo for image-copy. Yoke
95	// fan-out covers every vessel that ordain will visit.
96	
97	const RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS: &[&str] = &[
98	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_SENTRY_TETHER,
99	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE,
100	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_BOTTLE,
101	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_JUPYTER,
102	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_PLANTUML,
103	    RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_GRAFT,
104	    "rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo",
105	];
106	
107	// ── Hallmark-base locator construction ───────────────────────
108	//
109	// Module-local literals matching rbgc_Constants.sh values. Used to compose the
110	// airgap-bottle's RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR after the forge hallmark is captured.
111	// Orchestration writes a hallmark-namespace locator directly into the consumer
112	// vessel's rbrv.env; conjure resolves the locator at airgap-bottle build time.
113	// The forge hallmark's existence in GAR is established by ordain-forge's
114	// success — no separate enshrine validation step on the consumer.
115	
116	const RBTDRO_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS: &str = "rbi_hm";
117	const RBTDRO_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE: &str = "image";
118	
119	/// Slot 1 of the airgap-bottle vessel — the only base-image slot the airgap
120	/// supply chain populates from a hallmark.

content.text:
Yes, found it. Line 104 has a bare quoted string `"rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo"` while every other entry in `RBTDRO_YOKE_VESSEL_DIRS` is a named constant. This violates RCG's Constant Discipline — the file already has `RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_*` constants for the other five vessels, and the ccyolo path should follow the same pattern.

Want me to add `RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_CCYOLO` alongside the others (line 52-57) and reference it on line 104?

message.content: if this is a surgical change, make it now; do not commit.

content.text: Done. Added `RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_CCYOLO` alongside the other vessel-dir constants and replaced the bare literal in the array.

content: Goal was to fix an RCG Constant Discipline violation in rbtdro_onboarding.rs where a quoted vessel-dir literal sat among named constants. I added RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_CCYOLO and swapped it into the yoke array. Next: you decide whether to notch. (disable recaps in /config)
